spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles several edge cases (errors, fallbacks, feature detection, denials) — not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Page does not cite the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link — required self-heal anchor.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for local-network-access-restrictions
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Local Network Access restrictions. Address spaces: public, local, loopback. Permission-prompt model (no preflight in current Chrome impl). Secure context required for initiators. Fetch option targetAddressSpace: "local" to bypass mixed-content for declared local resources. Integrates with fetch, XHR, WebSocket, WebTransport, Service Worker windowClient.navigate. DNS-rebinding protection per connec...

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must cite chromestatus.com/feature/<id> in its 'see also' or references section — this is the self-heal anchor used by the slug cleanup script.

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
